title: Fix ip-range for classless subnet masks
body:
The `LastIPInSubnet` function worked only for classful subnet
masks (e.g. /8, /16, /24). For non standard subnet masks this
returned the wrong ip address.

This works now for all subnet mask. A unit test is added to
ensure this.

Fixes #8448

Do either of the other calls to FirstIPInSubnet need to be adjusted in a similar way?

```
./libpod/network/create.go:		firstIP, err := FirstIPInSubnet(ipRange)
./libpod/network/netconflist.go:		first, err := FirstIPInSubnet(ipRange)
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat Not sure what you are asking here?

I have added unit tests for `FirstIPInSubnet` and `LastIPInSubnet` and they now both work as I would expect.

@luap99 IDK for sure, subnet isn't in my wheelhouse.  My question was in this line https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8451/files#diff-2bc6787b85a93a48f8fceba3a0715a93577015b6addfa7edf49e6f00bc513c83R57 in subnet.go, you stopped calling FirstIPInSubnet.   The create.go and netconflist.go both still call that.  Do they need to have any adjustments too?  I think not for netconflist.go, but I'm less sure about create.go.

`ones == bits` only happens for a /32 subnet. A /32 subnet only contains a single IP. So we can just return the original IP here. The other calls to `FirstIPInSubnet` are correct since we really want the first IP there.  /32 is the only a special case where the fist ip equals last ip.
